channel.title: Software-Development on Abdulkadir Güngör
channel.description: Recent content in Software-Development on Abdulkadir Güngör

item.title: Abstract Class vs Interface: In-depth Technical Analysis and Architectural Decision Processes
item.description: In software architecture, “Abstraction” is a fundamental mechanism for managing complexity and ensuring the extensibility of a system. In object-oriented programming (OOP) languages, the two main carriers of this mechanism are Abstract Class and Interface structures. Although many developers think of these two structures as alternatives to each other, in technical depth, the differences between them have critical effects on design patterns and system performance.

item.title: Software Development with Python — A Comprehensive Technical Guide from Beginner to Expert
item.description: Python, published by Guido van Rossum in 1991, has become one of the most widely used programming languages in the world today. It consistently ranks in the top three on the TIOBE index; it is the de facto industry standard in diverse fields such as data science, web development, automation, and artificial intelligence. It is appropriate to start with the syntax that provides this popularity: Python has a readable structure close to English. However, this simplicity does not hide the power underlying the language. Integration with C extensions, support for asynchronous programming, an extensive standard library, and a massive ecosystem—all of these make Python a truly multi-layered language.

item.title: Lazy, Eager, and Explicit Loading: Avoiding the "N+1 Problem" with Data Loading Strategies
item.description: In modern software architectures, database interactions play a decisive role in the scalability and response time of an application. One of the biggest performance bottlenecks encountered, especially when using Object-Relational Mapping (ORM) tools, is the N+1 Query Problem. To overcome this issue and optimize the data access layer, it is essential to have a deep understanding of Lazy, Eager, and Explicit Loading strategies.

item.title: Generic Programming: Building Flexible and Reusable Structures Without Compromising Type Safety
item.description:
Generic programming is one of the cornerstones of modern software engineering. Subjecting code to logical abstraction independent of data types not only reduces development costs but also increases system stability by bringing runtime errors to compile-time.

1. Theoretical Foundations of Generic Programming

Generic programming is based on the principle that algorithms and data structures take the data types they will operate on as “parameters.” This approach is called Templates in the C++ world and Generics in the Java and C# worlds. The main goal is to avoid rewriting the same logic for different types, applying the “Don’t Repeat Yourself” (DRY) principle at the highest level.
